items.text: Guess you won't need my self-introduction. Aww, I spent so much time on it.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Rover... Rover!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I shattered Scar's illusion. That should've injured him.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Hehe, should I thank you for showing me mercy?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Stay away from {Male=him;Female=her}!

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Ugh. Didn't you promise to leave me some alone time with Rover?

items.speaker: Mysterious Woman in Red
items.text: With one condition. I do hope you haven't let that slip from your mind.

items.speaker: Mysterious Woman in Red
items.text: Don't let your improvisation trouble others.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Ugh... Look at you, rushing in to protect your precious "Perfect Movement".

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: No worries. I know all the dos and don'ts. I don't need you to tell me what to do.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Rover, it seems our happy little date... must come to an end.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: But don't forget my sincere reminders.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Let me know what you'll choose: an unexpected gift, an honest and open exchange, or a highly risky gamble...

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: The choice is yours. I know how smart you are. You won't make a hasty decision.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: We'll meet again... in the not-too-distant future.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: They are gone. Should we go after them?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Oh... Don't worry. I'm okay.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Scar's Resonance Abilities appear to involve teleportation and the manipulation of space. He trapped me in confinement, and I could only sense that it wasn't far from here.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: It took me some time to break that barrier. Sorry, Rover, if only I could've reached you sooner...

options.text: You are safe now, and that's all I care.
I broke free from it all thanks to you.

items.speaker: Strange Tacet Discord
items.text: B-brother... Brother...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Eh? Why are you here, little one?

options.text: It probably hid out of fear.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I've never seen any Tacet Discord display such vivid emotions...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The 'brother' it mentioned... Could it be...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Yes, that is a possibility, since Scar had been here before...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Let's talk about it later. Rover, can you fill me in on what happened?
You tell Yangyang about what just happened and the leads you gathered.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: It seems all the tragedies in Qichi Village were linked to that "ritual“...

options.text: ...And Scar was feeding me bits of info.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Making up stories based on real life to support his twisted beliefs... That does sound like something he might do...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: We cannot take his words for granted. Now we must locate where the "ritual" took place and see for ourselves.

options.text: Yes, we should give it a check.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Hmm? What's the matter?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Two distinct frequencies of Tacet Discords... I see.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I can feel it in the Streams... Over there. Follow me.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (In the south...)

items.speaker: Sanhua
items.text: They are unharmed. Please rest assured.

items.speaker: Sanhua
items.text: As you anticipated, Scar did not try to kill them... Yes, I stayed out of it like you asked.

items.speaker: Sanhua
items.text: Is everything alright on your end?

items.speaker: Sanhua
items.text: ...Please be sure to stay safe, My Lady.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Sorry, Rover. I didn't feel comfortable down there.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The air was heavy in that place, without any wind to speak of, but I could still feel so many emotions and desperate cries for help... I... I couldn't handle it.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The villagers were a complex mix of emotions... hope, resentment, and despair. And as I read the diary, I could feel the intense sorrow and longing of its owner.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: What was the diary's owner longing for? Was it the peaceful life she once had, or did she miss her only family—the person who pushed her towards such tragic end?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: ...My apologies. I got lost in thoughts again.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I wish I could say such tragedies won't happen again...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: ...But I still lack the confidence to make that claim.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: It was tragedies like this that made me want to become an Outrider... to become someone with the strength to stop them from ever happening again.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I cannot stop the Lament, but at least I should do everything in my power to help those affected by it.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Really? Thank you... Rover.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Dwelling on it won't do us any good... Let's go. I'll compile a report on what had happened here, and send it in along with the clues we've gathered.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Yes. Let's go back... together.
